title.en: Good heavens! Oh my goodness! 어머나! 세상에!

description.en: Learn exclamations used when you see something surprising or unbelievable. Feel the real vibe of "Eomeona" and "Sesange"!
description.ko: 깜짝 놀라거나 믿기 힘든 광경을 보았을 때 쓰는 감탄사들을 배워봅니다. "어머나"와 "세상에"의 리얼한 맛을 살려보세요!

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up guys. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 5화까지 오신 여러분 정말 대단해요. 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! You guys are amazing for making it to episode 5. I'm Mina.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I have a little surprise for you. Look at this photo on my phone.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어?
content.translation: Huh?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어머나~!
content.translation: Oh my goodness~!

insight.title: 어머나
insight.usage_tip: Often used by women or in a cute way when startled.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "세상에~!" 이게 정말 존 씨 어릴 때 사진이에요?
content.translation: Oh my goodness~! Is this really you when you were a baby. John?

insight.title: 세상에
insight.usage_tip: Used like 'In the world!' or 'Good heavens!' for big surprises.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yes. I was a very chubby baby. Right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "맙소사...," 너무 귀여워서 못 믿겠어요!
content.translation: Oh my goodness..., you were so cute. I can't believe it!

insight.title: 맙소사
insight.usage_tip: Often used for shocking or unbelievable situations.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Today. We are learning those "Good heavens" moments.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I hear "어머나" a lot in K-dramas. Is it only for women.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 음..., 주로 여자들이 많이 쓰긴 해요.
content.translation: Hmm..., it is mostly used by women.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 하지만 누구나 깜짝 놀랐을 때 "어머~!" 라고 할 수 있어요.
content.translation: But anyone can say 'Eomeo~!' when they are surprised.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 어머나~!
content.translation: Goodness~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: What about "세상에". It sounds very dramatic.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "세상에"는 "세상에 이런 일이!" 라는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: 'Sesange' means 'Such a thing in the world!'

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 믿기 힘든 일을 보거나 들었을 때 써보세요.
content.translation: Try using it when you see or hear something hard to believe.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "세상에~!"
content.translation: Oh my heavens~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: And you also said "맙소사". When do we use that.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "맙소사"는 약간..., 'Holy cow' 같은 느낌이에요.
content.translation: 'Map-so-sa' feels a bit like... 'Holy cow'.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 좋은 일에도 쓰지만. 어이없는 실수를 했을 때도 많이 써요.
content.translation: You can use it for good things. But also when you make a ridiculous mistake.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's try a situation. Imagine I accidentally dropped your birthday cake on the floor.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 헉!
content.translation: Gasps!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "어머나~! 어떡해~!"
content.translation: Oh my goodness~! What should I do~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm so sorry. It just slipped from my hands.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "세상에...," 케이크가 다 망가졌어요!
content.translation: My heavens..., the cake is totally ruined!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'll go buy another one right now.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "맙소사...," 오늘 제 생일 파티는 끝났네요!
content.translation: Oh my goodness..., my birthday party is over today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wait. Look closer. It's actually a fake cake made of plastic!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "세상에~!" 진짜인 줄 알고 울 뻔했잖아요!
content.translation: My heavens~! I almost cried because I thought it was real!

content.speaker: John
content.text: These expressions really add flavor to the conversation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 감정을 듬뿍 담아서 말하는 게 포인트예요!
content.translation: Exactly. The point is to speak with lots of emotion!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "어머나~!"
content.translation: Oh my goodness~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "어머~!"
content.translation: Oh my~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "세상에~!"
content.translation: Good heavens~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "맙소사...,"
content.translation: Oh my god...,

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "세상에 이런 일이!"
content.translation: What in the world!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 깜짝 놀랄 일이 생기면 꼭 외쳐보세요. "세상에~!"
content.translation: Everyone. If something surprising happens. Make sure to shout! 'Sesange~!'

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 어머나. 벌써 시간이 이렇게 됐네요. (Oh my. It's already this time.)
세상에. 정말 믿기지 않아요. (My heavens. I really can't believe it.)
맙소사. 열쇠를 집에 두고 왔어요. (Oh my god. I left my keys at home.)
이 강아지 정말 귀엽네요. (This puppy is really cute.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be ruined. To be broken
vocabulary.example: 제 휴대폰이 망가졌어요. (My phone is broken.)
너무 슬퍼서 울고 싶어요. (I'm so sad that I want to cry.)
